commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: This PR needs maintainer review for the session-routing upgrade impact, but there is no confirmed narrow code defect for a repair lane.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression found; the diff touches Telegram routing helpers and tests without changing dependencies, workflows, secrets handling, or install scripts. (sha=8e91dd70c483c64dba8c87baba525c88a35e31fe)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a Telegram forum-topic hint from supergroup `is_topic_message` to forum resolution call sites and regression tests for routing when `chat.is_forum` is omitted.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main already uses `is_topic_message` in the sequential lane but later `resolveTelegramForumFlag` ignores it and can return false when `chat.is_forum` is absent and `getChat` is unavailable or fails.
commands.review_followup: This PR needs maintainer review for the session-routing upgrade impact, but there is no confirmed narrow code defect for a repair lane.

target.title: Fix Telegram topic identity without forum flag
target.body:
## Summary
- Treat supergroup `is_topic_message` metadata as positive Telegram forum-topic evidence before falling back to `getChat`.
- Reuse the same forum hint in Telegram sequential lane keys, inbound message context, native commands, callback handling, and edited-message reply-chain tracking.
- Add regressions for missing `chat.is_forum` topic routing and for preserving DM-topic/non-forum behavior.

## Root Cause
Telegram topic identity was recovered inconsistently. The early sequential key path treated `is_topic_message: true` as topic evidence, but later inbound routing paths only trusted `chat.is_forum` or a successful `getChat` lookup. If Telegram omitted `chat.is_forum` and lookup was unavailable or failed, a real forum topic could collapse to the base group session and then be serialized with other topics.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Telegram forum-topic messages that include `is_topic_message: true` but omit `chat.is_forum` keep their `message_thread_id` in routing/session identity.

Real environment tested: Local temporary worktree at `/tmp/openclaw-telegram-topic-identity.xVbG0k` on branch `telegram-topic-identity-parallel`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `CI=1 pnpm install`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.topic-agentid.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.topic-agentid.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest reported `Test Files  3 passed (3)` and `Tests  124 passed (124)`. Both extension production and extension-test `tsgo` commands exited 0.

Observed result after fix: A supergroup message with `is_topic_message: true`, `message_thread_id: 3`, and no `chat.is_forum` now resolves topic config with thread id `3` and keeps `telegram:group:-1001234567890:topic:3` in the session key.

Live Telegram proof after update:
- Real environment tested: local Gateway runtime `2026.5.19` after the Telegram topic patch was applied by update overlay.
- Exact command or manual steps: checked `openclaw status --json` and filtered `openclaw logs --json --limit 1600 --max-bytes 1000000` for redacted Telegram topic inbound/outbound diagnostics.
- Evidence after fix:
  - `openclaw status --json` reported separate live session keys for `agent:main:telegram:group:-1003826723328:topic:8185`, `...:topic:5907`, `...:topic:8428`, and `...:topic:24`; recent topic sessions had `aborted=false`.
  - Gateway logs showed real Telegram inbound routes staying topic-qualified: `telegram:group:-1003826723328:topic:8185` at `2026-05-18T18:34:53.789-04:00` and `telegram:group:-1003826723328:topic:5907` at `2026-05-18T18:32:44.838-04:00`.
  - Telegram outbound logs showed replies delivered to matching topic thread ids: `threadId=8185` at `2026-05-18T18:35:06.712-04:00` and `threadId=5907` at `2026-05-18T18:32:59.297-04:00`.
  - A diagnostic sample also showed two Telegram topic sessions active at once: `topic:24(processing/embedded_run,...)|topic:5907(processing/embedded_run,...)` at `2026-05-18T17:44:55.514-04:00`.
- Observed result after fix: live Telegram forum topics remained topic-qualified in session inventory, inbound route logging, and outbound reply thread ids.

What was not tested: Crabbox/TDLib video proof, raw Bot API update-shape capture, callback/native-command live flows, and DM-topic live behavior were not run for this first patch. Focused unit tests cover the branch semantics; live logs cover post-update forum-topic routing and replies.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.topic-agentid.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`

Autoreview was attempted with `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --reviewer codex --fallback-reviewer none`, but nested Codex review could not inspect the local diff because bubblewrap failed with `bwrap: loopback: Failed RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted`; I manually reviewed the final diff after that blocker.
target.author: vacinc

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
